prompt: Play this short three-step table-state chain.

steps.prompt: Step 1: Hero is on the button and villain is in the big blind. Who is in position?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The button acts after the big blind preflop and keeps position postflop.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. On the button against the big blind, hero acts last after the flop, so hero has the in-position edge for the whole postflop hand.
steps.why_v1: Position means acting later, and the button has that edge here.

steps.prompt: Step 2: Keep the same seats. Hero opened preflop and villain called. Who has initiative on the flop?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. Hero raised last preflop, so hero brings the initiative to the flop.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Hero opened preflop, so hero is the last aggressor and carries initiative into the flop until the line changes.
steps.why_v1: Initiative usually belongs to the player who made the last aggressive action.

steps.prompt: Step 3: Now the flop comes Jh Th 9c. Which action matches the more pressure-building texture?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This coordinated two-tone flop builds pressure quickly.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Jh Th 9c is coordinated and two-tone, so it creates straight and flush pressure that fits the stronger pressure-building action.
steps.why_v1: More draws and connected ranks make this board more dynamic and pressure-building.
